title: [Kangaskhan] Battle Guide
summary: A [Normal]-type physical attacker whose [scrappy] ability lets [Normal] and [Fighting] moves hit [Ghost] types. [fake-out] lead play is the core tempo win condition.
strengths: [fake-out] steals the lead turn for free tempo
weaknesses: [Fighting] 2x weakness is common in the metagame

recommendedMoves.reason: Priority patch for the Speed problem

bodyMarkdown:
## Overview
[Kangaskhan] is a [Normal]-type physical attacker whose hidden ability [scrappy] allows [Normal] and [Fighting] moves to hit [Ghost] types. 105/80/80 balanced bulk with 95 Attack and 90 Speed is average on paper, but the combination of [fake-out] tempo and [scrappy] utility makes it a reliable lead on non-Mega teams in the Champions format.

## Strengths
[scrappy] is the defining ability because it eliminates the [Ghost] immunity that would otherwise wall the [Normal] STAB. [double-edge] therefore pressures every switch-in regardless of type, and [fake-out] steals one turn of lead tempo that can snowball into an early KO. [sucker-punch] patches the middling Speed tier by scoring priority KOs on weakened fast threats, and 105 HP supports repeated entries across a long match.

## Weaknesses & Answers
[Fighting] is a 2x weakness and common coverage, especially [close-combat] and [dynamic-punch]. Attack 95 underperforms against bulky walls, and Speed 90 loses to upper-tier sweepers above 100. [double-edge] recoil accumulates, so long matches require careful HP management. Priority users that outrun [sucker-punch] prediction also win the priority mind game.

## Recommended Moveset
[double-edge] is the locked [Normal] STAB for maximum damage, accepting the recoil. [earthquake] handles [Steel], [Rock], and [Electric] walls that resist [Normal] attacks. [sucker-punch] provides a priority slot for cleanup against weakened fast threats, and [fake-out] is the lead turn tempo move. A [return] variant exists but sacrifices the damage ceiling.

## Item · Ability · Tera
[silk-scarf] is the default item because the 20% [Normal] move boost directly scales [double-edge] damage. [life-orb] is the flat 1.3x alternative that also boosts [earthquake]. [sitrus-berry] is a mid-game heal for longer entries. Ability is [scrappy] — early-bird and inner-focus are not competitive. Tera [Normal] maxes [double-edge] damage, Tera [Fighting] flips the [Fighting] weakness.

## Teammates & Synergy
[Kangaskhan] is a [fake-out] lead, so pair it with setup sweepers that need a safe first turn. [Psychic], [Fairy], or [Ghost] partners cover the [Fighting] weakness, and a cleaner behind [Kangaskhan] converts [double-edge] chip into KOs. [stealth-rock] support widens the OHKO ranges, and hazard removal keeps [Kangaskhan] healthy across multiple entries.

## Play Patterns
Lead with [Kangaskhan], [fake-out] turn one, then [double-edge] or [earthquake] on the weakened target. Use [sucker-punch] against faster cleaners, and Tera on a telegraphed [Fighting] move. Switch out before recoil becomes lethal and reenter later once HP recovers.
